data.title: Imugene applies for ASX quotation of additional ordinary shares
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Imugene ( (AU:IMU) ) has issued an announcement.

Imugene Limited has applied to the ASX for quotation of 17,829 new ordinary fully paid shares, to be issued on 11 January 2026 following the conversion or exercise of existing options or other convertible securities. The modest increase in quoted securities slightly expands Imugene’s share capital base and reflects routine capital management activity, with limited immediate impact on ownership structure or day-to-day operations.

The application, lodged as a new announcement under Appendix 2A of the ASX Listing Rules, confirms that the additional shares will rank as ordinary equity alongside existing IMU securities. While small in scale, such incremental issuances are part of the company’s broader approach to funding and incentivising stakeholders within the competitive biotechnology market.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:IMU) stock is a Hold with a A$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Imugene stock, see the AU:IMU Stock Forecast page.

More about Imugene

Imugene Limited is an Australia-listed biotechnology company focused on developing novel immunotherapies, with its ordinary fully paid shares traded on the ASX under the code IMU. The company operates in the biopharmaceutical sector, where equity issuances and option exercises are common tools to support ongoing research, development, and corporate activities.

Average Trading Volume: 1,646,802

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$51.77M

data.title: Sunda Energy Targets Transformational New Zealand Oil and Gas Deal Backed by £6.7m Fundraise
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Baron Oil ( (GB:SNDA) ).

Sunda Energy has agreed to acquire Matahio Energy NZ, gaining 100% ownership of a suite of oil and gas production and exploration permits in New Zealand’s onshore Taranaki Basin that produced about 1,000 boepd in 2025. The assets, which include 2.6 MMboe of 2P reserves and multiple low-risk development and drilling opportunities, are expected to provide material cash flow in a stable OECD jurisdiction with government incentives for gas development.

To fund the deal, Sunda has arranged a complex fundraising of up to £6.7 million, combining a firm subscription, a convertible loan note facility with Alumni Capital, director-backed subscriptions, and a retail offer, alongside a share consolidation. The acquisition, seen by management as transformational and complementary to existing Timor-Leste and Philippine positions, remains subject to shareholder approval at an April general meeting and New Zealand regulatory clearance, and is intended to strengthen the company’s scale, diversification and growth prospects for investors and host-country stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:SNDA) stock is a Hold with a £0.03 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Baron Oil stock, see the GB:SNDA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SNDA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SNDA is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by weak financial performance (no revenue, widening losses, and ongoing cash burn despite low leverage). Technicals provide some support from recent price strength, but overbought signals temper that. Valuation remains challenged because profitability is negative and no dividend support is indicated.

To see Spark’s full report on SNDA stock, click here.

More about Baron Oil

Sunda Energy Plc is an AIM-quoted exploration and appraisal company focused on gas assets in the Asia-Pacific region. The group is building a diversified upstream portfolio across Timor-Leste, the Philippines and now New Zealand, targeting a mix of production, development and exploration assets to gain critical mass as an independent regional energy player.

Average Trading Volume: 106,868,590

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £11.8M

data.title: Jardine Matheson Cancels 15,000 Shares After Market Buyback
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Jardine Matheson Holdings ( (GB:JAR) ) is now available.

Jardine Matheson Holdings Limited has repurchased 15,000 of its ordinary shares on 7 April 2026 at a price of US$73.175 per share, with the shares to be cancelled rather than held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company’s issued share capital stands at 293,102,530 ordinary shares, each carrying one vote, a figure shareholders can use to determine whether they must disclose changes in their ownership stakes under UK transparency rules.

The cancellation of the repurchased shares marginally reduces Jardine Matheson’s share count, which can be incrementally supportive of earnings per share and existing shareholders’ proportional ownership over time. By providing an updated total of voting rights, the company is also facilitating regulatory compliance and enhancing transparency for investors who track their disclosure thresholds under the Financial Conduct Authority’s guidance.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:JAR) stock is a Buy with a $94.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Jardine Matheson Holdings stock, see the GB:JAR Stock Forecast page.

More about Jardine Matheson Holdings

Jardine Matheson Holdings Limited is a diversified Asian-focused conglomerate with interests spanning sectors such as retail, property, hotels, motor vehicles, and other services across key regional markets. The group operates through multiple listed subsidiaries and affiliates, providing a broad portfolio of consumer and commercial services to customers and businesses in Asia and beyond.

Mitie Group plc ( (GB:MTO) ) has issued an announcement.

Mitie Group plc has continued to execute its share buyback programme, repurchasing 175,000 ordinary shares at 175 pence each through Peel Hunt on 7 April 2026, with the company intending to cancel the shares. Following settlement and cancellation, Mitie will have 1,312,723,525 ordinary shares in issue, excluding treasury shares, marginally increasing earnings per share and consolidating voting rights for remaining investors.

The transaction forms part of the group’s ongoing capital management strategy, leveraging its strong UK market position in facilities management and compliance services. By reducing its share count while maintaining its operational focus on high-performing, ESG-led services, Mitie underlines its confidence in its long-term prospects and its commitment to shareholder returns.

data.description:

THG ( (GB:THG) ) has provided an announcement.

THG PLC has published its Annual Report & Accounts for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, making the document available on its website and through the U.K. Financial Conduct Authority’s National Storage Mechanism. The annual report will also be distributed to shareholders, providing detailed financial and operational information for the period.

The company has scheduled its 2026 Annual General Meeting for 1:00 p.m. on 24 June 2026 at THG Studios in Altrincham, with a formal notice to be sent to investors in due course. The publication of the report and confirmation of the AGM timetable underscore THG’s adherence to U.K. listing and disclosure requirements, giving shareholders a defined opportunity to review performance and engage in governance matters.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial quality (negative EBIT and a deterioration to negative operating/free cash flow in 2025) and a technically weak setup (below key moving averages with negative MACD). These are partially offset by a very low P/E and a moderately constructive earnings call focused on de-leveraging, liquidity extension, and reiterated guidance despite near-term revenue and cost headwinds.

data.title: Austral Resources Sees Major Institutional Holder Fall Below Substantial Threshold After Share Issue
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Austral Resources Australia Limited ( (AU:AR1) ).

Industry Super Holdings Pty Ltd and its controlled entities have notified Austral Resources Australia Limited that they have ceased to be substantial shareholders following the company’s issue of additional shares, which diluted their holding below the 5% substantial holder threshold. The change reflects a shift in the company’s share register rather than a disposal of the underlying 122,561,570 shares, and includes adjustments in associate relationships, potentially altering the influence of institutional investors over corporate voting and governance dynamics.

The notice clarifies that Industry Super Holdings and related entities’ relevant interest is now reduced in percentage terms because of the expanded capital base, signaling a more dispersed ownership structure at Austral Resources. This development may have implications for control and future strategic decision‑making at the miner, as the relative voting power of this institutional group diminishes and the composition of influential shareholders evolves.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:AR1) stock is a Hold with a A$0.08 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Austral Resources Australia Limited stock, see the AU:AR1 Stock Forecast page.

More about Austral Resources Australia Limited

Austral Resources Australia Limited is an Australian resources company focused on mining and producing copper, operating within the broader metals and mining sector. The company’s activities center on exploration and development of copper assets, serving industrial customers exposed to global commodity and base metals markets.

Average Trading Volume: 15,377,331

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$207.1M

Find detailed analytics on AR1 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
